question.text: An associated person's NISM certification expired 2 months ago, but the person continues to handle client transactions. What is the compliance implication?

answer.text: The person is in violation of SEBI (CAPSM) Regulations as no associated person can continue specified activities without a valid certificate
There is no violation as there is a standard 6-month grace period after expiry
The violation only matters if a client lodges a complaint
The intermediary can grant a temporary exemption pending revalidation

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Under SEBI (CAPSM) Regulations, 2007, no associated person engaged in specified activities shall continue to be so engaged unless such person holds a valid certificate. There is no grace period after expiry — the certificate must be revalidated BEFORE it expires. **The Trap:** There is no automatic grace period and the intermediary cannot grant exemptions. The responsibility is on both the individual and the intermediary to ensure valid certification. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Compliance officers should maintain a certification tracker for all associated persons and initiate revalidation at least 12 months before expiry (the CPE completion window).
